repo_prior_art.cite: exp_saturation_skew_threshold_v1 studies a scalar saturation moment program, not covariance implementability for network assignment laws

published_prior_art.cite: ThiyageswaranKokotBrennanMeilaYuFazel2026 optimizes the covariance SDP, but does not state this two-block simultaneous relaxed/implementable symmetrization

ThiyageswaranKokotBrennanMeilaYuFazel2026 gives SDP plus Gaussian rounding; VivianoLeiImbensKarrerSchrijversShi2026 gives graph cluster-design objectives, not zero-loss elliptope attainability by P_cut
HarshawSavjeSpielmanZhang2024 motivates robustness-balanced implementable designs, but not exact optimality of I_n for the interference-homophily SDP

repo_prior_art.cite: pid_network_exposure_v1 and pid_network_random_set_v1 are shared-law feasibility lessons for exposure mappings, not elliptope-vs-sign-covariance design gaps

published_prior_art.cite: ChenLiDengWang2024 notes covariance-legitimacy constraints and HuberMaric2017 gives the symmetric Bernoulli cut-polytope substrate, but neither proves a family-specific positive implementability gap for this causal design objective
HuberMaric2017 characterizes realizability abstractly and GoemansWilliamson1995/Nesterov-style rounding gives one-sided approximation structure, not the sharp r_star/rho_star phase certificate

soundness_flags.label: ass:balanced-sign-design
soundness_flags.one_line: HarshawSavjeSpielmanZhang2024 supports Gram-Schmidt Walk robustness/balance tradeoffs, not a standard condition named balanced Rademacher randomization with global sign symmetry for every admissible law.

soundness_flags.label: ass:schatten-two-robustness
soundness_flags.one_line: The Schatten covariance penalty is supported by the Thiyageswaran et al. SDP framework, not by Harshaw et al. as cited.

soundness_flags.label: ass:balanced-sign-design
soundness_flags.one_line: The condition P(Z=z)=P(Z=-z) already implies E_P[Z_i]=0, so the assumption should drop the redundant zero-mean clause or explain why both are separately retained.

accepted_scope_caveats.label: oeq:sharp-phase-boundary
accepted_scope_caveats.caveat: The exact r_star boundary and rho_star rounding-loss certificate are posed as an open-ended target, not as a delivered theorem.
accepted_scope_caveats.bound_claim: The field-tier kernel is the conditional package of symmetry reduction, two exact corners, and a positive gap window; the fully sharp iff phase diagram remains the named Stage 0 construction route.

strengthening_paths.suggestion: Retag ass:balanced-sign-design as a proposal-specific design restriction, or replace the Harshaw cite with a source that actually imposes global sign-symmetric half-treatment margins.
Move the Schatten-2 robustness citation to ThiyageswaranKokotBrennanMeilaYuFazel2026 and anchor it to the section/equation where the Schatten exponent and covariance penalty are introduced.
Add explicit candidate formulas or named dual programs for r_cut, kappa_iid, r_gap^- and r_gap^+ so the corner and gap theorems are not carried by unnamed frontier symbols.
Add one worked m=2 or m=3 block-symmetric feasibility exhibit computing E_m^blk, C_m^pm, and Delta_m^pm on the same primitives to make the implementability gap auditable before full proof.

recommended_next_step: Continue revise on this angle: the novelty kernel clears the field floor, but the standard-assumption tags need correction before Stage 0.
